feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Where tf is the ammo in this game
entry.content: I am playing on hard and I very quickly end up in situations where I will have 0 ammo and realistically cannot survive a fight. It is otherwise not a very hard game and I do not have the impression I am wasting ammo or mussing a ridiculous amount of shots

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: “Triangle of Sadness” director Ruben Östlund reveals next movie’s plot details
entry.content: >“The Entertainment System Is Down” is a dark comedy disaster drama set aboard a long-haul flight from London to Sydney, starring Keanu Reeves, Kirsten Dunst, Woody Harrelson, Samantha Morton, Daniel Brühl, Joel Edgerton and Nicholas Brau, among others. A key group of the passengers are boisterous Australian cricket fans returning home after Australia has lost a series to England. Östlund said the characters were inspired partly by the legend of Australian cricketer David Boon, who reportedly dr

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: got caught by my dad getting ready to fap to the bunny suits in Trails Beyond the Horizon:
entry.content: I'm visiting my dad for a few weeks, brought my PS5 and am currently playing Trails Beyond the Horizon... and of course, I have everyone in the bunnysuits from the DLC. The only TV I had available is in the downstairs living room, and... MY DAD WALKED IN AND SAW A CUTSCENE WITH ALL THE GIRLS STANDING AROUND IN THE BUNNY SUITS, WITH THEIR ASSES AND TITS HANGING OUT... and I was getting ready to fap. He asked me "Why on Earth are they dressed like that?" I just said "That's Japan for you," but my

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Corny! Corny!! CORNY!!!!!!
entry.content: This is a real, genuine corny hillbilly maizefield bust down. Do the Corn Dog Twist. Canned corn slurry & calabash salad, calamari with garlic-corn rice sliders. Canadian corn whiskey from a corn foam-lined cask with canary island corn chili dressing. Fried corn casserole with baked aioli corn sluice. Calabrian corn tacos served after a Corsican ritual corn dance done to a corny polka beat.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Is there any good Ninja game?
entry.content: I've never played a ninjagame and i'm in a ninja mood. Any good ninja game? No i don't wanna play Samurai shit like Sekiro, i want actual ninja agile stuff with ninja stars and all the ninja stuff, not MUH HONOLABLE SWOLD *sepuku* I wanted to play Sifu but seems more karate chinky shit rather than actual black dressed ninja fun. I just want to be ninja and take chow chow down to chinatown.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Electrical engineer here. Am I retarded for thinking that Claude is absolute dog…
entry.content: Electrical engineer here. Am I retarded for thinking that Claude is absolute dog shit compared to chatgtp when it comes to anything but coding? Maybe im retarded and dont know how to utilize it correctly.. My company is making everyone use claude and tracking our usage, I just upload a datasheet into it and watch it eat a million tokens just to answer what voltage is on a pin(I could have ctrl f it quicker) and when I need AI to read through datasheets or help me do analysis I just us my private

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Rat vs Bird
entry.content: Saw someone shitting on xfce in another thread and praising lxqt as being lighter weight so decided to actually compare the two. I installed both DEs on separate installs of three different operating systems (Arch, Debian, and Fedora). I'll post exactly what I did in case someone wants to reproduce my steps and verify, alongside screenshots I took of the outcomes. All VM's had 2 cores and 4GB of memory. All VM's were updated and the only extra installed software was fastfetch and cowsay. Notice

entry.title: /mu/ Music: David Byrne: Grown Backwards [Nonesuch, 2004] The two opera selections signify o…
entry.content: David Byrne: Grown Backwards [Nonesuch, 2004] The two opera selections signify one thing, and it's not that those voice lessons have finally paid off. It's that more even than Randy Newman or Tom Waits (or Sting), this likable Manhattan progressive conceives himself as a performer of artsongs. As a writer of same he has his moments. Somebody somewhere could do justice to the absurdly abject "Glad" or the smarmily rationalized "Empire" or "She Only Sleeps," the love tribute of a sex worker's boyf

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Can Someone Please Explain What The Fuck Was Mulholland Drive?
entry.content: Mulholland Drive isn't just about Hollywood, it's a twisted spiritual parable. Think of the classic non-dual teaching: in the dark, you mistake a rope for a snake and tremble in fear. When the light comes, you see it's just a rope, and the fear dissolves. That is supposed to be liberation. David Lynch inverts that entirely. Club Silencio is where the "light" gets turned on. "No hay banda" no band, no grand orchestral score. The terrifying snake of the dream (the mobsters, the hitman, the mystery
